examples: By capping her grades at 94 while most other teachers grade on a scale that tops out at 100, your teacher could jeopardize a student's chance of getting a scholarship or getting into a top college.
He would never do anything to jeopardize his career.
Moreover, Beijing does not wish to jeopardize its investments in the Iranian energy sector.
She said there was concern from governments that a ".africa" domain could jeopardize the existing ccTLDs.
